title: Limonium donegalense
intro: Limonium donegalense, also known as Donegal sea lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the coast of Ireland. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory conditions, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Limonium donegalense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Limonium donegalense is classified within the family Plumbaginaceae, commonly known as the plum family. This family consists of approximately 35 genera and 1,000 species of flowering plants. Limonium donegalense is further classified as a species within the Limonium genus, which comprises around 100 species of coastal plants. The genus is native to the Mediterranean region, with many species being found in coastal areas of Europe, Africa, and Asia. Limonium donegalense is a rare and endangered species found in Ireland.
